summary: Story 1.4a is well scoped, sourced, and testable for the diary record schema foundation, but it is blocked by an invalid executor assignment for a primary database/schema story.

template_compliance.notes: Required sections are present: Status, Executor Assignment, Story, Acceptance Criteria, Integration Verification, CodeRabbit Integration, Tasks / Subtasks, Dev Notes, Testing, Change Log, Dev Agent Record, and QA Results. No obvious template placeholders were found.

executor_assignment.notes: The story is primary type Database and isolates migrations, models, relationships, and factories. The validation task and story template map Schema/DB/RLS/Migrations work to executor @data-engineer with quality_gate @dev. The story currently sets executor @dev and quality_gate @data-engineer, and @data-engineer is not a valid quality_gate in validate-next-story's allowed quality gate set.

goal_context_clarity.notes: The story value, split-slice interpretation, dependency on Story 1.3, and downstream dependency for Stories 1.4b/1.4c are explicit.

technical_guidance.notes: The story identifies migrations, tables, columns, indexes, model relationships, constants, factories, Tenant relationship, uuid route identity, and explicit no-service/no-policy/no-UI boundaries.

reference_effectiveness.notes: Claims were cross-checked against docs/prd.md Story 1.4, docs/architecture.md memory_records, memory_tags, memory_record_tag, and memory_highlights sections, framework coding standards, and existing memory migrations/models.

self_containment.notes: A database-focused executor has enough context to implement the schema/model foundation without inventing service, policy, route, Livewire, media, sharing, approval, or AI classification behavior.

testing_guidance.notes: Focused PHPUnit schema/model coverage, existing memory route/profile regression tests, route inspection, and Pint formatting are specified with Sail-prefixed commands.

security_scope.notes: Tenant-owned persistence, author/editor columns, tenant-scoped tag uniqueness, and exclusions for public sharing, visibility expansion, approval, media, and AI classification are clear.

coderabbit_integration.notes: CodeRabbit is enabled in core-config.yaml and the story includes analysis, agents, quality gates, self-healing, and schema/security focus areas. The local data-engineer agent is used for database review instead of the older @db-sage wording in the checklist.

code_intelligence.notes: isCodeIntelAvailable returned false in this session.

anti_hallucination.notes: No invented product features were found. The split intentionally reserves period fields while excluding period behavior, matching architecture and MVP scope.

dev_readiness.notes: Implementation should not start until the executor assignment is corrected to the database-story mapping.

blockers.title: Invalid executor assignment for schema foundation story
blockers.evidence: Story 1.4a declares Primary Type: Database and scopes work to migrations, models, relationships, and factories, but Executor Assignment is executor @dev and quality_gate @data-engineer.
blockers.required_fix: Update Executor Assignment to executor @data-engineer and quality_gate @dev, keeping quality_gate_tools non-empty and aligned to the focused schema/model and regression commands.

nice_to_have: After correcting 1.4a, validate 1.4b and 1.4c in dependency order before any implementation starts.
Jira child story sync remains pending; local story artifacts are the source of truth until PO/DevOps explicitly sync them.
recommended_command: /agent aiox-sm Address PO-BLOCK-1.4A-001 in docs/stories/1.4a.diary-record-schema-foundation.story.md, then return to PO for *validate-story-draft
